title: selliguea tomentosa
intro: Selliguea tomentosa, also known as Polypodium tomentosum or Pilea tomentosa, is a species of plant belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly referred to as the spleenwort fern. Selliguea tomentosa is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating cough and respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Selliguea tomentosa include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are typically prepared by boiling the leaves in water or mixing them with other medicinal plants to enhance their therapeutic effects.
